2606.07108.title: DyCon: Dynamic Reasoning Control via Evolving Difficulty Modeling
2606.07108.paper_summary: Recent advances in Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) demonstrate remarkable performance improvements by iteratively reflecting, exploring, and executing complex tasks, yet suffer from inefficiencies due to redundant reasoning, known as "overthinking". Existing methods to mitigate this issue either rely on static difficulty estimates or require task-specific training, and thus fail to adapt to the dynamic complexity during reasoning. In this work, we empirically show that the problem difficulty evolves dynamically throughout the reasoning process and is linearly encoded in the LRM's step-level embeddings. Building on this insight, we propose DyCon, a training-free framework that leverages latent step-level representations to explicitly model the evolving task difficulty, enabling the dynamic control of reasoning depth to mitigate the overthinking issue. Extensive experiments conducted on four models ranging from 4B to 32B, and across twelve benchmarks in math reasoning, general question answering, and coding tasks demonstrate that DyCon significantly enhances reasoning efficiency by reducing redundant steps without sacrificing accuracy or generalization. Project page and code are available at https://github.com/yu-lin-li/DyCon.
2606.07108.paper_summary_zh: 最近在大型推理模型（LRMs）方面的進展顯示，通過迭代反思、探索和執行複雜任務，性能有了顯著提升，但由於冗餘推理而導致效率低下，這被稱為「過度思考」。現有的方法要麼依賴靜態的難度評估，要麼需要特定任務的訓練，因此無法適應推理過程中的動態複雜性。在這項工作中，我們實證表明，問題的難度在整個推理過程中是動態演變的，並且在線性編碼的LRM步驟級嵌入中體現出來。基於這一見解，我們提出了DyCon，一個無需訓練的框架，利用潛在的步驟級表示來明確建模演變中的任務難度，從而實現推理深度的動態控制，以減輕過度思考的問題。在四個模型（範圍從4B到32B）和十二個基準測試（包括數學推理、一般問答和編程任務）上進行的廣泛實驗表明，DyCon顯著提高了推理效率，通過減少冗餘步驟而不犧牲準確性或泛化能力。項目頁面和代碼可在 https://github.com/yu-lin-li/DyCon 獲得。
2606.07108.author: Tengyao Tu et.al.
2606.07108.authors: Tengyao Tu, Yulin Li, Hui-Ling Zhen, Libo Qin, Zhoujun Wei, Jinghua Piao, Zhuotao Tian, Yong Li, Min Zhang
